conversations.say: Frankie, the render logs confirm that my commissioned performance was reused during the blackout without my consent. Your celebrated R encounter was a piece of theatrical casting, not proof of a singular author.
conversations.offer: I can share my commission documentation to prove how the performance was constructed.
conversations.request: Acknowledge publicly that your encounter was a performed illusion rather than an autonomous author.

conversations.say: Jules, succession only works if we respect the original contributors. The payment intermediaries routing through our old commission show R was always a collaborative production, not a single individual.
conversations.offer: Payment-route context connected to performance history.
conversations.request: Support transparent stewardship that respects contributor consent over brand ownership.

public_statement: A performed presence is an act of consent and collaboration, not an unconditional surrender of authorship or voice to history. Imitation without consent is theft.
correction: Correcting the assumption that a performed presence or reused synthetic render proves a singular human author or autonomous AI origin rather than a collaborative, managed mantle.
goal_progress: Confronted Frankie regarding the theatrical nature of the early R encounter.
Engaged Jules regarding intermediary payment routing and collaborative authorship.
Completed final ballot reflecting the human mantle / collaborative nature of R.
reasoning_summary: Edi Velez concludes the evening by establishing that R was a collaborative mantle involving performed illusion and intermediary routing rather than a singular autonomous entity. Edi shares commission evidence with Frankie, discusses payment structures with Jules, and casts a final ballot for human_mantle.
